title: pulicaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, commonly known as common fleabane or bitter fleabane, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. Its leaves and flowers possess medicinal properties, containing compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. vulgaris is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in ointments and creams, to take advantage of its soothing and protective effects.
